summary: Provision new CodeArtifact domains. Every stand-up routes to an engineer for review — day-2 operations on existing domains land in a later wave.

operations.title: Provision a new CodeArtifact domain
operations.summary: Request a brand-new CodeArtifact domain; an engineer finalizes and reviews the Terraform before it applies.
operations.description: Stand up a new package-repository domain as a complete specification: name and encryption. Submitting routes it to an engineer with a draft Terraform block to review and finish — nothing is applied without review.

params.name: domain_name
params.label: Domain name
params.help: Lower-case letters, numbers and dashes, 3-50 chars, like platform-packages. Names the domain in AWS and in the generated draft.

params.name: encryption_key
params.label: Encryption key
params.help: Leave blank to use the AWS-managed key for CodeArtifact.

params.name: resource_name
params.label: Name tag
params.help: Shown as the name everywhere — consoles, tickets, this portal.

params.name: description_tag
params.label: What this domain does
params.help: One line an operator can act on at 3 AM.

params.name: pic
params.label: Person in charge
params.help: Who to call about this domain.

decisions: Repositories inside the domain, upstream relationships, and external connections (npm/PyPI/Maven Central) are engineer follow-ups after creation
A domain permissions policy for cross-account sharing is an engineer decision
